transcript.text: Hello, here's a video request. What do I think libraries are going to be like in the future?
I think you said between like 10 and 50 years in the future.
And I don't even know. He left it on one of my videos.
So, yeah, because in the future in the libraries, I don't think they're going to have books.
Like right now, all the space in libraries is books.
And I'm actually kind of surprised how long it's taking for people to make the transition into audiobooks and e-books and stuff.
You still have the Barnes & Noble with all those books, you know, and you go in the library and all those books.
Because there's still a bunch of old people, you know, like in the old world, you know, like Bill O'Reilly, you know,
Thinking that marijuana should be illegal and bigoted against strip clubs and all this kind of stuff.
And those guys are like reading books.
But in the future, it's not going to be books.
But so libraries are going to transform into, you know, libraries since the beginning have been centers of learning.
And who was it who set up most a lot of libraries in America?
Was it Rockefeller?
And so the design of the centers of learning, you know, all of that space that is now taken up by books will be a little bit different, won't it?
Libraries will be kind of like museums.
They'll be, they'll kind of, you know, become really similar to museums.
It'll be a kind of like interactive museum.
There'll be places where you go and you look around on the wall, like my timeline, my history timeline was going to be on the walls of all the libraries in the future.
And you can quote me on that, boy.
Um, so you'd have different wings representing like different parts of the world or different levels or whatever.

And along the wall, my timeline would be along the wall and, uh, and then all the other different educational maps, like, um, you know, like all of the different, uh, bills that, that were passed within Congress.

And then, and then, you know, um, but it would be totally objective when, well, I'm sorry, I was, I was, this isn't like my library.
I'm saying general libraries, but, um, yeah, there would be, uh, just all kinds of like educational type artwork.

So they'll have words and letters and numbers and it'll be something that when you look at, you'll help, it'll, it'll, it'll be, you'll be learning and getting information from it.
Um, kind of like my timeline.
I look at my timeline, it's not done yet, but when it's done yet, it'll be kind of like artwork, like beautiful to look at, you know, um, captivating.
